Home / Alternatives to Shopify / Shopify vs Medusa

Shopify vs Medusa

A side-by-side look at Shopify (the paid SaaS) and Medusa (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Shopify Medusa
Tagline Hosted e-commerce platform for online stores. Open source alternative to Shopify for developers.
License Proprietary SaaS MIT
Pricing Basic from $29/month plus transaction fees. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 3/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

JavaScript-first teams wanting a modern headless commerce stack.

Medusa strengths

  • Node.js-native — familiar for JS teams.
  • Modular — plug in any storefront.
  • Rapid community growth.

Medusa weaknesses

  • Younger ecosystem than Saleor/WooCommerce.
  • Some modules still maturing.
  • Plugins community-driven.

What's the catch with Shopify?

  • App ecosystem pushes costs higher than advertised.
  • Transaction fees if you skip Shopify Payments.
  • Checkout customization is limited on lower plans.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Shopify: see Shopify alternatives, or learn more about Medusa on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
